description: OWASP top 10, common vulnerabilities, CTF methodology, cryptography basics, and network security
source: OWASP Top 10 (2021), NIST Cybersecurity Framework, MITRE ATT&CK, standard CTF/pentesting curriculum

triples.subject: SQL injection
triples.object: user input is concatenated into SQL query without sanitization

triples.subject: SQL injection
triples.object: data exfiltration, authentication bypass, remote code execution

triples.subject: blind SQL injection
triples.object: boolean conditions or time delays in responses

triples.subject: UNION-based SQL injection
triples.object: UNION SELECT to extract data from other tables

triples.subject: XSS
triples.object: malicious JavaScript into web pages viewed by other users

triples.subject: XSS
triples.object: output encoding and Content Security Policy

triples.subject: Content Security Policy
triples.object: which scripts can execute on a page

triples.subject: CSRF
triples.object: authenticated user's browser into making unintended requests

triples.subject: buffer overflow
triples.object: program writes data beyond allocated memory buffer

triples.subject: ASLR
triples.object: memory addresses of stack, heap, and libraries

triples.subject: return-oriented programming
triples.object: existing code gadgets ending in ret instructions

triples.subject: horizontal privilege escalation
triples.object: another user's resources at same privilege level

triples.subject: SUID binary
triples.object: owner's privileges regardless of who runs it

triples.subject: social engineering
triples.object: humans into revealing information or performing actions

triples.subject: phishing
triples.object: fraudulent emails or websites mimicking trusted entities

triples.subject: pretexting
triples.object: scenario to manipulate target into providing access

triples.subject: broken access control
triples.object: unauthorized users to access restricted resources

triples.subject: OWASP Top 10 (2021)
triples.object: A08: Software and Data Integrity Failures

triples.subject: OWASP Top 10 (2021)
triples.object: A09: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures

triples.subject: SSRF
triples.object: server into making requests to internal resources

triples.subject: symmetric encryption
triples.object: same key for encryption and decryption

triples.subject: asymmetric encryption
triples.object: public key for encryption, private key for decryption

triples.subject: TLS
triples.object: asymmetric encryption for key exchange, symmetric for data transfer

triples.subject: TLS handshake
triples.object: shared session key between client and server

triples.subject: cryptographic hash function
triples.object: preimage resistance, collision resistance, avalanche effect

triples.subject: MD5
triples.object: collision resistance (should not be used for security)

triples.subject: bcrypt
triples.object: password hashing with configurable work factor

triples.subject: password hashing
triples.object: salt to prevent rainbow table attacks

triples.subject: enumeration
triples.object: open ports, services, and attack surface

triples.subject: nmap
triples.object: network port scanning and service detection

triples.subject: network security
triples.object: firewalls to filter traffic by rules

triples.subject: IDS
triples.object: suspicious network activity via signatures or anomalies

triples.subject: IPS
triples.object: IDS with ability to block detected threats

triples.subject: defense in depth
triples.object: multiple security controls so failure of one doesn't compromise system

triples.subject: principle of least privilege
triples.object: minimum permissions needed for a task

triples.subject: zero trust architecture
triples.object: no implicit trust based on network location

triples.subject: zero trust architecture
triples.object: continuous verification of identity and authorization

triples.subject: command injection
triples.object: user input is passed to OS shell command unsanitized

triples.subject: command injection
triples.object: avoid shell calls; use language APIs with argument arrays

triples.subject: path traversal
triples.object: ../ sequences to access files outside intended directory

triples.subject: path traversal
triples.object: canonicalize paths and validate against allowed directory
